user_query: Change childs third day, second breakfast option to spagetti with maple syrup

updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 3 > Breakfast > Avocado Toast — Garlic-Ginger Combo

nutrition_note.from: Well-balanced morning option with fiber from whole-grain bread, heart-healthy fats from avocado, and protein from egg. Lemon adds vitamin C; this combo provides steady energy and satiety. Note: garlic/ginger are not included in the listed ingredients.
nutrition_note.to: A high-carbohydrate, sweet breakfast option: spaghetti provides quick energy from starches while maple syrup adds concentrated sugars. Adding butter or walnuts increases fat and calories and can add satiety and some healthy fats from nuts. Best served occasionally rather than daily due to high simple-sugar content.

short_description.from: Avocado toast on whole-grain bread topped with egg and a lemon finish.
short_description.to: Spaghetti tossed with pure maple syrup for a sweet, comforting breakfast twist.

meal_suggestion.from: Toast the bread, mash avocado and spread on top, add a cooked egg, and finish with a squeeze of lemon and a dash of pepper. Optional flavor tweaks include adding garlic, ginger, or chili flakes if you like.
meal_suggestion.to: Cook spaghetti until tender according to package instructions. Drain and return to the pot. While still warm, drizzle with pure maple syrup and toss to coat. For extra richness, stir in a small knob of butter or a splash of milk. Optionally sprinkle with a pinch of salt and a dusting of cinnamon, or top with chopped walnuts for texture. Serve warm.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the second breakfast option from an avocado toast variant into a new meal 'Spaghetti with Maple Syrup' as requested. All meal fields rewritten to reflect the new dish and its preparation and nutrition profile.
